)]}'

Quiz\",4,[[[\"a474cd35-6c21-4e72-94a0-c38b5491b449\"]],[[\"735fcfef-9fbd-4c89-9789-6a9760587bec\"]],[[\"4d3f7b07-e9e6-43d3-ab8b-184fa27a9f1e\"]],[[\"a5ec927b-12eb-45d1-989f-12eb3db4ce53\"]],[[\"c361a555-5c2d-42e2-94d0-a65da95be660\"]],[[\"48f71a82-08d3-46fa-a37f-d657fb2f0723\"]],[[\"d6ce2ec3-f98a-4529-acd5-08bff271cb3b\"]],[[\"ef358221-3904-4dbc-be6f-e1e8dea63954\"]]],3,null,null,null,null,[\"\",[2,null,null,\"en\",null,null,null,[2,2],true]],[1768346092,144365000],null,null,null,null,[1768346040,459674000],null,null,null,1],[\"575a9e5d-40fb-44a4-b2d3-21a573bdb547\",\"Reverse Engineering an AI Coding Agent\",1,[[[\"a474cd35-6c21-4e72-94a0-c38b5491b449\"]],[[\"735fcfef-9fbd-4c89-9789-6a9760587bec\"]],[[\"4d3f7b07-e9e6-43d3-ab8b-184fa27a9f1e\"]],[[\"a5ec927b-12eb-45d1-989f-12eb3db4ce53\"]],[[\"c361a555-5c2d-42e2-94d0-a65da95be660\"]],[[\"48f71a82-08d3-46fa-a37f-d657fb2f0723\"]],[[\"d6ce2ec3-f98a-4529-acd5-08bff271cb3b\"]],[[\"ef358221-3904-4dbc-be6f-e1e8dea63954\"]]],3,null,[null,[null,null,null,[[\"a474cd35-6c21-4e72-94a0-c38b5491b449\"],[\"735fcfef-9fbd-4c89-9789-6a9760587bec\"],[\"4d3f7b07-e9e6-43d3-ab8b-184fa27a9f1e\"],[\"a5ec927b-12eb-45d1-989f-12eb3db4ce53\"],[\"c361a555-5c2d-42e2-94d0-a65da95be660\"],[\"48f71a82-08d3-46fa-a37f-d657fb2f0723\"],[\"d6ce2ec3-f98a-4529-acd5-08bff271cb3b\"],[\"ef358221-3904-4dbc-be6f-e1e8dea63954\"]],\"en\",true],\"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/notebooklm/AG60hOpf_6VVqB5uKfezMgWKMLY6DmHqogOnybD7aG7Qphrd0vwh4LhRvRAM-cJOJULLY0f5sgWuLKjpwl3K4EsfWlIh1_DrJoNK5dH0JNXpOu3F_PLThad5Ri3DLUqXbPLpJMZS22k8yK4sVEv-8iYfJkh9pWNAEBc\\u003dm140\",\"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/notebooklm/AG60hOpf_6VVqB5uKfezMgWKMLY6DmHqogOnybD7aG7Qphrd0vwh4LhRvRAM-cJOJULLY0f5sgWuLKjpwl3K4EsfWlIh1_DrJoNK5dH0JNXpOu3F_PLThad5Ri3DLUqXbPLpJMZS22k8yK4sVEv-8iYfJkh9pWNAEBc\\u003dm140-dv\",true,[[\"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/notebooklm/AG60hOpf_6VVqB5uKfezMgWKMLY6DmHqogOnybD7aG7Qphrd0vwh4LhRvRAM-cJOJULLY0f5sgWuLKjpwl3K4EsfWlIh1_DrJoNK5dH0JNXpOu3F_PLThad5Ri3DLUqXbPLpJMZS22k8yK4sVEv-8iYfJkh9pWNAEBc\\u003dm140-dv\",4,\"audio/mp4\"],[\"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/notebooklm/AG60hOpf_6VVqB5uKfezMgWKMLY6DmHqogOnybD7aG7Qphrd0vwh4LhRvRAM-cJOJULLY0f5sgWuLKjpwl3K4EsfWlIh1_DrJoNK5dH0JNXpOu3F_PLThad5Ri3DLUqXbPLpJMZS22k8yK4sVEv-8iYfJkh9pWNAEBc\\u003dm140\",1,\"audio/mp4\"],[\"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/notebooklm/AG60hOpf_6VVqB5uKfezMgWKMLY6DmHqogOnybD7aG7Qphrd0vwh4LhRvRAM-cJOJULLY0f5sgWuLKjpwl3K4EsfWlIh1_DrJoNK5dH0JNXpOu3F_PLThad5Ri3DLUqXbPLpJMZS22k8yK4sVEv-8iYfJkh9pWNAEBc\\u003dmm,hls\",2],[\"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/notebooklm/AG60hOpf_6VVqB5uKfezMgWKMLY6DmHqogOnybD7aG7Qphrd0vwh4LhRvRAM-cJOJULLY0f5sgWuLKjpwl3K4EsfWlIh1_DrJoNK5dH0JNXpOu3F_PLThad5Ri3DLUqXbPLpJMZS22k8yK4sVEv-8iYfJkh9pWNAEBc\\u003dmm,dash\",3]],[734]],null,null,null,[1768311531,99748000],null,null,null,null,[1768310985,133261000],null,null,null,1],[\"0b9bb718-2749-4d3e-8448-42c94019c6a5\",\"AI Agent Step-by-Step Evolution\",7,[[[\"a474cd35-6c21-4e72-94a0-c38b5491b449\"]],[[\"735fcfef-9fbd-4c89-9789-6a9760587bec\"]],[[\"4d3f7b07-e9e6-43d3-ab8b-184fa27a9f1e\"]],[[\"a5ec927b-12eb-45d1-989f-12eb3db4ce53\"]],[[\"c361a555-5c2d-42e2-94d0-a65da95be660\"]],[[\"48f71a82-08d3-46fa-a37f-d657fb2f0723\"]],[[\"d6ce2ec3-f98a-4529-acd5-08bff271cb3b\"]],[[\"ef358221-3904-4dbc-be6f-e1e8dea63954\"]]],3,null,null,null,null,null,[1768311181,982128000],null,null,null,[[null,\"en\",null,1,2],\"AI Agent Step-by-Step Evolution\",[[\"AI Agent Step-by-Step Evolution\",[\"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/notebooklm/AG60hOqFtx4K6wGyQv_kj3i-x5_obLDNEwSzSzwrbe2X1Ijpb2aS3Vh7FhjMbd42z8KzHKI63nlGA4omo5gg29w-v-J20EfFNgwXk1ldYw06nFK-Ah94xIX9UOtn2re549JILVOn9Mw1Oah-_z2ZhZ1tYgiwjX_Ares\\u003dw2752-d-h1536\",2752,1536],\"Infographic showing the five-stage evolution of a modern AI agent, progressing from a simple tool (v0) to a complex expert system (v4).\",\"TITLE\\nBuilding a Modern AI Agent: A Step-by-Step Evolution\\n\\nSTAGE 1: THE MINIMALIST AGENT (V0)\\nEstablishes that a single 'bash' tool is enough to read, write, search, and even create subagents.\\n[The path starts at a terminal command prompt icon.]\\n\\nSTAGE 2: THE CORE AGENT (V1)\\nIntroduces 4 essential tools (bash, read, write, edit) to form the basic, repeating agent loop.\\n[The path connects Stage 1 to a circular process involving four small icons representing the essential tools.]\\n\\nSTAGE 3: THE PLANNER (V2)\\nAdds a 'TodoWrite' tool to make plans explicit, tracking multi-step tasks to maintain focus.\\n[The flow moves to a blueprint/clipboard icon, where a process shows task planning with checkmarks and an arrow loop.]\\n\\nSTAGE 4: THE DELEGATOR (V3)\\nThe 'Task' tool spawns child agents with isolated context to handle complex jobs without confusion.\\n[The flow leads into a central mechanical gear icon, which represents the core engine. Below the engine, smaller agent icons branch off, illustrating delegation.]\\n\\nSTAGE 5: THE EXPERT (V4)\\n[The final stage shows the flow connecting to a large desktop monitor setup with an internal diagram of a processing brain.]\\n\\n- Concept: Tool\\n- What it is: CAN DO\\n- Example: 'bash', 'read_file'\\n\\n- Concept: Skill\\n- What it is: How an agent KNOWS TO DO it\\n- Example: A guide on PDF processing\\n\\nA 'Skill' tool loads domain expertise, educating the agent on \\\"how\\\" to perform specific tasks.\"]]],[1768311055,334226000],null,null,null,1],[\"835a5938-4a60-43a0-b2c3-6f01b435664a\",\"Agent Architecture Quiz\",4,[[[\"a474cd35-6c21-4e72-94a0-c38b5491b449\"]],[[\"735fcfef-9fbd-4c89-9789-6a9760587bec\"]],[[\"4d3f7b07-e9e6-43d3-ab8b-184fa27a9f1e\"]],[[\"a5ec927b-12eb-45d1-989f-12eb3db4ce53\"]],[[\"c361a555-5c2d-42e2-94d0-a65da95be660\"]],[[\"48f71a82-08d3-46fa-a37f-d657fb2f0723\"]],[[\"d6ce2ec3-f98a-4529-acd5-08bff271cb3b\"]],[[\"ef358221-3904-4dbc-be6f-e1e8dea63954\"]]],3,null,null,null,null,[\"\",[2,null,null,\"en\",null,null,null,[2,2],true]],[1768311105,796819000],null,null,null,null,[1768311059,696595000],null,null,null,1],[\"fdd20d4a-f422-42b3-896c-60997035f4ca\",\"Study Guide for \\\"Learn Claude Code\\\"\",2,[[[\"a474cd35-6c21-4e72-94a0-c38b5491b449\"]],[[\"735fcfef-9fbd-4c89-9789-6a9760587bec\"]],[[\"4d3f7b07-e9e6-43d3-ab8b-184fa27a9f1e\"]],[[\"a5ec927b-12eb-45d1-989f-12eb3db4ce53\"]],[[\"c361a555-5c2d-42e2-94d0-a65da95be660\"]],[[\"48f71a82-08d3-46fa-a37f-d657fb2f0723\"]],[[\"d6ce2ec3-f98a-4529-acd5-08bff271cb3b\"]],[[\"ef358221-3904-4dbc-be6f-e1e8dea63954\"]]],3,null,null,[\"# Study Guide for \\\"Learn Claude Code\\\"\\n\\nThis study guide is designed to assess and reinforce understanding of the concepts, architecture, and philosophy behind the AI agent development tutorial presented in the \\\"learn-claude-code\\\" repository. It covers the progressive evolution of the agent through its five versions, from a minimal bash-based implementation to a sophisticated system incorporating skills and subagents.\\n\\n## Short-Answer Quiz\\n\\n*Instructions: Provide a concise answer (2-3 sentences) for each of the following questions based on the provided source material.*\\n\\n1.  What is the core philosophy of the `v0_bash_agent.py` and how does it implement subagents?\\n2.  According to the `v1_basic_agent.py` documentation, what is the fundamental secret of modern AI agents, and what are the four essential tools that cover 90% of use cases?\\n3.  Explain the problem of \\\"Context Fade\\\" that `v2_todo_agent.py` is designed to solve. How does the `TodoWrite` tool address this issue?\\n4.  What is \\\"Context Pollution,\\\" and how does the subagent mechanism introduced in `v3` solve this problem?\\n5.  Differentiate between a \\\"Tool\\\" and a \\\"Skill\\\" as defined in the `v4` documentation.\\n6.  What is the paradigm shift of \\\"Knowledge Externalization\\\" introduced in `v4`? Why is it considered a more democratic approach than traditional model training?\\n7.  Describe the three layers of \\\"Progressive Disclosure\\\" used by the `v4` skills mechanism. What is the purpose of this layered approach?\\n8.  According to the `v4` documentation on \\\"Caching Economics,\\\" why is it more cost-effective to inject skill content as a `tool_result` rather than modifying the system prompt?\\n9.  What is the purpose of the `Agent Type Registry` in the `v3` subagent mechanism? Name the three default agent types and their primary functions.\\n10. Describe the core agent loop pattern that, according to the repository's `README.md`, all coding agents share.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Answer Key\\n\\n1.  The core philosophy of `v0` is \\\"Bash is All You Need.\\\" It posits that a single `bash` tool is sufficient for all agent capabilities, including reading, writing, and searching files. Subagents are implemented through recursive self-calls, where the agent executes `python v0_bash_agent.py \\\"subtask\\\"` as a bash command, spawning an isolated child process with a fresh context.\\n2.  The fundamental secret is that \\\"The Model IS the Agent,\\\" meaning the model itself is the primary decision-maker, and the code simply provides tools and a loop. The four essential tools are `bash` (run any command), `read_file` (view file contents), `write_file` (create or overwrite files), and `edit_file` (make surgical changes to files).\\n3.  \\\"Context Fade\\\" is the problem where an agent loses track of its plan during complex, multi-step tasks because the plan exists only in the model's transient memory. The `TodoWrite` tool solves this by making the plan explicit and visible as a structured to-do list, allowing both the user and the model to track progress and maintain focus.\\n4.  \\\"Context Pollution\\\" occurs when a single agent's message history becomes cluttered with intermediate details from one subtask (like file exploration), leaving little context window space for subsequent subtasks (like refactoring). The `v3` subagent mechanism solves this by delegating tasks to child agents with isolated, fresh message histories, which then return only a concise summary to the parent agent.\\n5.  A \\\"Tool\\\" is a capability, defining what the model *CAN do* (e.g., `bash`, `read_file`). A \\\"Skill\\\" is knowledge, defining how the model *KNOWS to do* a domain-specific task (e.g., PDF processing, code review). Tools provide the agent with actions, while skills provide it with expertise.\\n6.  \\\"Knowledge Externalization\\\" is the shift from storing knowledge within a model's parameters (requiring expensive training to modify) to storing it in external, human-editable documents like `SKILL.md`. This is more democratic because anyone can \\\"teach\\\" the agent by simply editing a text file, eliminating the need for ML expertise, GPU clusters, and large training datasets.\\n7.  The three layers are: Layer 1 (Metadata), which includes the skill's name and description and is always loaded; Layer 2 (`SKILL.md` body), which contains detailed instructions and is loaded on trigger; and Layer 3 (Resources), which includes optional scripts and reference files. This approach keeps the agent's initial context lean while allowing for arbitrary depth of knowledge on-demand.\\n8.  Injecting skill content as a `tool_result` appends it to the conversation history, which preserves the exact prefix of previous messages. This allows API providers to use a KV Cache for the unchanged prefix, drastically reducing computation and cost. Modifying the system prompt invalidates the entire cache, forcing an expensive re-computation of the full context every time.\\n9.  The `Agent Type Registry` defines different types of subagents with specific capabilities by filtering their available tools and providing them with a specialized system prompt. The three default types are `explore` (a read-only agent for searching and analysis), `code` (a full agent with all tools for implementation), and `plan` (a read-only agent for designing strategies without modifying files).\\n10. The core agent loop is a `while True` loop where the agent repeatedly cycles through a simple process. First, the model is called with the current message history and available tools. If the model's response includes tool calls, those tools are executed, the results are appended to the message history, and the loop continues. The loop only terminates when the model's response does not request a tool use.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Essay Questions\\n\\n*Instructions: The following questions are designed for longer, more detailed analysis. No answers are provided.*\\n\\n1.  Analyze the architectural evolution of the agent from v0 to v4. Discuss how each version's \\\"Core Insight\\\" (e.g., \\\"Bash is All You Need,\\\" \\\"Structured Planning,\\\" \\\"Knowledge Externalization\\\") directly addresses a specific limitation of the previous version.\\n2.  The v4 documentation makes a strong case for \\\"Knowledge Externalization\\\" as a paradigm shift from \\\"training AI\\\" to \\\"educating AI.\\\" Elaborate on this argument, detailing the practical and economic differences between modifying agent behavior via fine-tuning versus editing a `SKILL.md` file.\\n3.  Discuss the importance of \\\"Context Caching Economics\\\" in the design of cost-effective and scalable AI agents. Explain the concept of a KV Cache, identify at least three anti-patterns described in the documentation that invalidate the cache, and articulate the principles of the recommended \\\"append-only\\\" architecture.\\n4.  The v2 documentation states, \\\"Structure constrains AND enables.\\\" Using the `TodoManager` from v2 and the `Agent Type Registry` from v3 as primary examples, explain how imposing specific rules and limitations on an AI agent's behavior can lead to more focused, reliable, and capable systems for complex tasks.\\n5.  Compare and contrast the two methods of implementing subagents presented in the repository: the implicit, recursive process spawning in `v0_bash_agent.py` and the explicit `Task` tool in `v3_subagent.py`. Evaluate the trade-offs between these two approaches in terms of simplicity, control, context isolation, and overall capability.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Glossary\\n\\n| Term | Definition |\\n| :--- | :--- |\\n| **Agent** | An AI system that operates in a loop, repeatedly using tools to interact with an environment until a task is complete, rather than just providing a single text response. |\\n| **Agent Skills Spec** | An official specification that agents like Kode CLI, Claude Code, and Cursor can support, likely related to the format and use of Skills. |\\n| **Agent Type Registry** | A configuration in v3 that defines different types of subagents (e.g., `explore`, `code`, `plan`) with specialized prompts and a limited set of tools to ensure focused behavior. |\\n| **Append-Only** | The recommended architectural pattern for managing conversation history, where new messages and tool results are always added to the end. This preserves the message prefix, enabling significant cost savings through KV Caching. |\\n| **Context Caching Economics** | The study of how different agent architectures impact API costs, primarily focusing on the use or invalidation of the provider-side KV Cache. |\\n| **Context Fade** | The problem where an agent loses track of its high-level plan during a long, multi-step task because the plan is not explicitly stored or tracked. Addressed in v2 with the `TodoManager`. |\\n| **Context Isolation** | The key principle behind the subagent mechanism, where a child agent is given its own fresh message history, preventing it from being influenced by or polluting the parent agent's context. |\\n| **Context Pollution** | The problem where a single agent's context window becomes filled with low-level details from one subtask (e.g., exploring files), degrading its performance on subsequent subtasks. Addressed in v3 with subagents. |\\n| **Knowledge Externalization** | A paradigm shift where domain-specific knowledge is stored in external, human-editable documents (`SKILL.md`) instead of being locked inside a model's trained parameters. This democratizes the process of customizing AI behavior. |\\n| **KV Cache** | A mechanism used by LLM providers to store the intermediate computational states (key-value pairs) of a prompt's prefix. If a subsequent API call uses the same exact prefix, the cached states can be reused, avoiding redundant computation and reducing costs. |\\n| **Progressive Disclosure** | A three-layer approach to loading skills that keeps the agent's context lean. It starts by loading only metadata (Layer 1), then the full skill body when triggered (Layer 2), and provides access to associated resources as needed (Layer 3). |\\n| **Skill** | A package of on-demand domain expertise that tells an agent *HOW* to perform a task. It is distinct from a tool, which is a raw capability. Skills are stored in `SKILL.md` files. |\\n| **SKILL.md** | A standardized file format for defining a Skill. It consists of YAML frontmatter for metadata (name, description) and a Markdown body containing detailed instructions, best practices, and code examples. |\\n| **Subagent** | A child agent spawned by a parent agent to perform a focused, isolated subtask. Subagents have their own context, system prompt, and a potentially filtered set of tools. |\\n| **Tool** | A function or capability that the model can call to interact with its environment. Examples include `bash`, `read_file`, and `write_file`. Tools define what an agent *CAN do*. |\\n| **TodoManager** | A class introduced in v2 to manage a structured, visible task list. It enforces constraints (e.g., only one task `in_progress`) to help the agent maintain focus on complex, multi-step projects. |\",[\"Study Guide\",\"Quiz with answer key plus glossary\",null,[[\"a474cd35-6c21-4e72-94a0-c38b5491b449\"],[\"735fcfef-9fbd-4c89-9789-6a9760587bec\"],[\"4d3f7b07-e9e6-43d3-ab8b-184fa27a9f1e\"],[\"a5ec927b-12eb-45d1-989f-12eb3db4ce53\"],[\"c361a555-5c2d-42e2-94d0-a65da95be660\"],[\"48f71a82-08d3-46fa-a37f-d657fb2f0723\"],[\"d6ce2ec3-f98a-4529-acd5-08bff271cb3b\"],[\"ef358221-3904-4dbc-be6f-e1e8dea63954\"]],\"en\",\"You are a highly capable research assistant and tutor. Create a detailed study guide designed to review understanding of the sources. Create a quiz with ten short-answer questions (2-3 sentences each) and include a separate answer key. Suggest five essay format questions, but do not supply answers. Also conclude with a comprehensive glossary of key terms with definitions.\",null,true],[[[[0,35,[[[0,35,[\"Study Guide for \\\"Learn Claude Code\\\"\"]]],[null,4]]],[35,416,[[[35,416,[\"This study guide is designed to assess and reinforce understanding of the concepts, architecture, and philosophy behind the AI agent development tutorial presented in the \\\"learn-claude-code\\\" repository. It covers the progressive evolution of the agent through its five versions, from a minimal bash-based implementation to a sophisticated system incorporating skills and subagents.\"]]]]],[416,433,[[[416,433,[\"Short-Answer Quiz\"]]],[null,5]]],[433,562,[[[433,562,[\"Instructions: Provide a concise answer (2-3 sentences) for each of the following questions based on the provided source material.\",[null,true]]]]]],[562,650,[[[562,597,[\"What is the core philosophy of the \"]],[597,613,[\"v0_bash_agent.py\",[null,null,null,null,null,null,null,true]]],[613,650,[\" and how does it implement subagents?\"]]],[null,1],null,[null,null,0,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,\"1.\",2,1,1]]],[650,818,[[[650,667,[\"According to the \"]],[667,684,[\"v1_basic_agent.py\",[null,null,null,null,null,null,null,true]]],[684,818,[\" documentation, what is the fundamental secret of modern AI agents, and what are the four essential tools that cover 90% of use cases?\"]]],[null,1],null,[null,null,0,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,\"2.\",2,2,2]]],[818,947,[[[818,861,[\"Explain the problem of \\\"Context Fade\\\" that \"]],[861,877,[\"v2_todo_agent.py\",[null,null,null,null,null,null,null,true]]],[877,913,[\" is designed to solve. How does the \"]],[913,922,[\"TodoWrite\",[null,null,null,null,null,null,null,true]]],[922,947,[\" tool address this issue?\"]]],[null,1],null,[null,null,0,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,\"3.\",2,3,3]]],[947,1048,[[[947,1026,[\"What is \\\"Context Pollution,\\\" and how does the subagent mechanism introduced in \"]],[1026,1028,[\"v3\",[null,null,null,null,null,null,null,true]]],[1028,1048,[\" solve this problem?\"]]],[null,1],null,[null,null,0,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,\"4.\",2,4,4]]],[1048,1128,[[[1048,1111,[\"Differentiate between a \\\"Tool\\\" and a \\\"Skill\\\" as defined in the \"]],[1111,1113,[\"v4\",[null,null,null,null,null,null,null,true]]],[1113,1128,[\" capability.\"]]],[null,1],null,[null,null,0,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,null,\"5.\",2,5,5]]],[7278,7360,[[[7278,7360,[\"\\n--------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\"]]]]],[7360,7368,[[[7360,7368,[\"Glossary\"]]],[null,5]]],[7368,10648,null,null,[17,2,[[7368,7382,[[7368,7372,[[7368,7372,[[[7368,7372,[\"Term\"]]]]]]],[7372,7382,[[7372,7382,[[[7372,7382,[\"Definition\"]]]]]]]]],[7382,7556,[[7382,7387,[[7382,7387,[[[7382,7387,[\"Agent\",[true]]]]]]]],[7387,7556,[[7387,7556,[[[7387,7556,[\"An AI system that operates in a loop, repeatedly using tools to interact with an environment until a task is complete, rather than just providing a single text response.\"]]]]]]]]],[7556,7710,[[7556,7573,[[7556,7573,[[[7556,7573,[\"Agent Skills Spec\",[true]]]]]]]],[7573,7710,[[7573,7710,[[[7573,7710,[\"An official specification that agents like Kode CLI, Claude Code, and Cursor can support, likely related to the format and use of Skills.\"]]]]]]]]],[7710,7900,[[7710,7729,[[7710,7729,[[[7710,7729,[\"Agent Type Registry\",[true]]]]]]]],[7729,7900,[[7729,7900,[[[7729,7800,[\"A configuration in v3 that defines different types of subagents (e.g., \"]],[7800,7807,[\"explore\",[null,null,null,null,null,null,null,true]]],[7807,7809,[\", \"]],[7809,7813,[\"code\",[null,null,null,null,null,null,null,true]]],[7813,7815,[\", \"]],[7815,7819,[\"plan\",[null,null,null,null,null,null,null,true]]],[7819,7900,[\") with specialized prompts and a limited set of tools to ensure focused behavior.\"]]]]]]]]],[7900,8137,[[7900,7911,[[7900,7911,[[[7900,7911,[\"Append-Only\",[true]]]]]]]],[7911,8137,[[7911,8137,[[[7911,8137,[\"The recommended architectural pattern for managing conversation history, where new messages and tool results are always added to the end. This preserves the message prefix, enabling significant cost savings through KV Caching.\"]]]]]]]]],[8137,8303,[[8137,8162,[[8137,8162,[[[8137,8162,[\"Context Caching Economics\",[true]]]]]]]],[8162,8303,[[8162,8303,[[[8162,8303,[\"The study of how different agent architectures impact API costs, primarily focusing on the use or invalidation of the provider-side KV Cache.\"]]]]]]]]],[8303,8499,[[8303,8315,[[8303,8315,[[[8303,8315,[\"Context Fade\",[true]]]]]]]],[8315,8499,[[8315,8499,[[[8315,8487,[\"The problem where an agent loses track of its high-level plan during a long, multi-step task because the plan is not explicitly stored or tracked. Addressed in v2 with the \"]],[8487,8498,[\"TodoManager\",[null,null,null,null,null,null,null,true]]],[8498,8499,[\".\"]]]]]]]]],[8499,8704,[[8499,8516,[[8499,8516,[[[8499,8516,[\"Context Isolation\",[true]]]]]]]],[8516,8704,[[8516,8704,[[[8516,8704,[\"The key principle behind the subagent mechanism, where a child agent is given its own fresh message history, preventing it from being influenced by or polluting the parent agent's context.\"]]]]]]]]],[8704,8932,[[8704,8721,[[8704,8721,[[[8704,8721,[\"Context Pollution\",[true]]]]]]]],[8721,8932,[[8721,8932,[[[8721,8932,[\"The problem where a single agent's context window becomes filled with low-level details from one subtask (e.g., exploring files), degrading its performance on subsequent subtasks. Addressed in v3 with subagents.\"]]]]]]]]],[8932,9183,[[8932,8957,[[8932,8957,[[[8932,8957,[\"Knowledge Externalization\",[true]]]]]]]],[8957,9183,[[8957,9183,[[[8957,9055,[\"A paradigm shift where domain-specific knowledge is stored in external, human-editable documents (\"]],[9055,9063,[\"SKILL.md\",[null,null,null,null,null,null,null,true]]],[9063,9183,[\") instead of being locked inside a model's trained parameters. This democratizes the process of customizing AI behavior.\"]]]]]]]]],[9183,9448,[[9183,9191,[[9183,9191,[[[9183,9191,[\"KV Cache\",[true]]]]]]]],[9191,9448,[[9191,9448,[[[9191,9448,[\"A mechanism used by LLM providers to store the intermediate computational states (key-value pairs) of a prompt's prefix. If a subsequent API call uses the same exact prefix, the cached states can be reused, avoiding redundant computation and reducing costs.\"]]]]]]]]],[9448,9709,[[9448,9470,[[9448,9470,[[[9448,9470,[\"Progressive Disclosure\",[true]]]]]]]],[9470,9709,[[9470,9709,[[[9470,9709,[\"A three-layer approach to loading skills that keeps the agent's context lean. It starts by loading only metadata (Layer 1), then the full skill body when triggered (Layer 2), and provides access to associated resources as needed (Layer 3).\"]]]]]]]]],[9709,9888,[[9709,9714,[[9709,9714,[[[9709,9714,[\"Skill\",[true]]]]]]]],[9714,9888,[[9714,9888,[[[9714,9774,[\"A package of on-demand domain expertise that tells an agent \"]],[9774,9777,[\"HOW\",[null,true]]],[9777,9873,[\" to perform a task. It is distinct from a tool, which is a raw capability. Skills are stored in \"]],[9873,9881,[\"SKILL.md\",[null,null,null,null,null,null,null,true]]],[9881,9888,[\" files.\"]]]]]]]]],[9888,10098,[[9888,9896,[[9888,9896,[[[9888,9896,[\"SKILL.md\",[true]]]]]]]],[9896,10098,[[9896,10098,[[[9896,10098,[\"A standardized file format for defining a Skill. It consists of YAML frontmatter for metadata (name, description) and a Markdown body containing detailed instructions, best practices, and code examples.\"]]]]]]]]],[10098,10275,[[10098,10106,[[10098,10106,[[[10098,10106,[\"Subagent\",[true]]]]]]]],[10106,10275,[[10106,10275,[[[10106,10275,[\"A child agent spawned by a parent agent to perform a focused, isolated subtask. Subagents have their own context, system prompt, and a potentially filtered set of tools.\"]]]]]]]]],[10275,10446,[[10275,10279,[[10275,10279,[[[10275,10279,[\"Tool\",[true]]]]]]]],[10279,10446,[[10279,10446,[[[10279,10379,[\"A function or capability that the model can call to interact with its environment. Examples include \"]],[10379,10383,[\"bash\",[null,null,null,null,null,null,null,true]]],[10383,10385,[\", \"]],[10385,10394,[\"read_file\",[null,null,null,null,null,null,null,true]]],[10394,10400,[\", and \"]],[10400,10410,[\"write_file\",[null,null,null,null,null,null,null,true]]],[10410,10439,[\". Tools define what an agent \"]],[10439,10445,[\"CAN do\",[null,true]]],[10445,10446,[\".\"]]]]]]]]],[10446,10648,[[10446,10457,[[10446,10457,[[[10446,10457,[\"TodoManager\",[true]]]]]]]],[10457,10648,[[10457,10648,[[[10457,10570,[\"A class introduced in v2 to manage a structured, visible task list. It enforces constraints (e.g., only one task \"]],[10570,10581,[\"in_progress\",[null,null,null,null,null,null,null,true]]],[10581,10648,[\") to help the agent maintain focus on complex, multi-step projects.\"]]]]]]]]]]]]]],null,null,null,1]],null,null,[1768311104,570557000],null,null,null,null,[1768311066,700355000],null,null,null,1],[\"173255d8-12b3-4c67-b925-a76ce6c71735\",\"Agent Flashcards\",4,[[[\"a474cd35-6c21-4e72-94a0-c38b5491b449\"]],[[\"735fcfef-9fbd-4c89-9789-6a9760587bec\"]],[[\"4d3f7b07-e9e6-43d3-ab8b-184fa27a9f1e\"]],[[\"a5ec927b-12eb-45d1-989f-12eb3db4ce53\"]],[[\"c361a555-5c2d-42e2-94d0-a65da95be660\"]],[[\"48f71a82-08d3-46fa-a37f-d657fb2f0723\"]],[[\"d6ce2ec3-f98a-4529-acd5-08bff271cb3b\"]],[[\"ef358221-3904-4dbc-be6f-e1e8dea63954\"]]],3,null,null,null,null,[\"\",[1,null,null,\"en\",null,null,[2,2],null,true]],[1768311102,683814000],null,null,null,null,[1768311058,331340000],null,null,null,1],[\"7e91cd58-3aff-422d-aebc-d3d6b9f551e6\",\"Evolution an educational video for high school students (ages 14-18) about Claude Code and AI agents. Make it visually engaging and easy to understand. Explain: What are AI agents, how they work (the loop of thinking, taking action, observing results), what tools agents use, how Claude Code helps developers write code, and why this matters for the future. Use simple language and make it interesting for teenagers considering tech careers.\",null,1,1],\"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/notebooklm/AG60hOqB52ITTMfQg37e4T7p4Ob-s76K6gdupfCfT-xhC1vVp9gzY1lmyDXquYh6qDWFfz1BK9wSYXbqhxruqHH_dyWIjHfiW04kIVpZdxybPDL9DLVeYPKRbZTrzZKBz2-SPaojwSyi4fyWOQ-jb-XlkZVEqBj28lM\\u003dm22-dv\",[[\"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/notebooklm/AG60hOqB52ITTMfQg37e4T7p4Ob-s76K6gdupfCfT-xhC1vVp9gzY1lmyDXquYh6qDWFfz1BK9wSYXbqhxruqHH_dyWIjHfiW04kIVpZdxybPDL9DLVeYPKRbZTrzZKBz2-SPaojwSyi4fyWOQ-jb-XlkZVEqBj28lM\\u003dm22\",1,\"video/mp4\"],[\"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/notebooklm/AG60hOqB52ITTMfQg37e4T7p4Ob-s76K6gdupfCfT-xhC1vVp9gzY1lmyDXquYh6qDWFfz1BK9wSYXbqhxruqHH_dyWIjHfiW04kIVpZdxybPDL9DLVeYPKRbZTrzZKBz2-SPaojwSyi4fyWOQ-jb-XlkZVEqBj28lM\\u003dmm,hls\",2],[\"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/notebooklm/AG60hOqB52ITTMfQg37e4T7p4Ob-s76K6gdupfCfT-xhC1vVp9gzY1lmyDXquYh6qDWFfz1BK9wSYXbqhxruqHH_dyWIjHfiW04kIVpZdxybPDL9DLVeYPKRbZTrzZKBz2-SPaojwSyi4fyWOQ-jb-XlkZVEqBj28lM\\u003dmm,dash\",3],[\"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/notebooklm/AG60hOqB52ITTMfQg37e4T7p4Ob-s76K6gdupfCfT-xhC1vVp9gzY1lmyDXquYh6qDWFfz1BK9wSYXbqhxruqHH_dyWIjHfiW04kIVpZdxybPDL9DLVeYPKRbZTrzZKBz2-SPaojwSyi4fyWOQ-jb-XlkZVEqBj28lM\\u003dm22-dv\",4,\"video/mp4\"]],[413,291666000]],null,[1768269821,415555000],null,null,null,null,[1768268991,469299000],null,null,null,1],[\"848df2ec-4916-4dea-aa20-3dc02954cfd0\",\"Building a presentation slide deck for high school students (ages 14-18) explaining how Claude Code and AI agents work. Structure: 1) Title slide - 'How AI Agents Like Claude Code Work', 2) What is AI vs AI Agents - key differences, 3) The Agent Loop - think, act, observe, repeat, 4) Tools and Capabilities - what agents can do, 5) Claude Code Demo - how it helps programmers, 6) Building Your Own Agent - simplified steps from the v0-v4 code examples, 7) Future of AI Agents, 8) Getting Started - resources for curious students. Use simple language, engaging visuals, and relatable examples.\",\"en\",1,1],\"Building Digital Workers\",[[[\"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/notebooklm/AG60hOpZAAZUXqZloCo_YFfVGZeP2IufHHZEQLZyPnWcEqIi_iRdXTZrAlM_6KQsK3YAbHeODVxzh3YQsEw-xT_BxZ9sZ6OdJCu7Qfld6xIY_KajRi0xPjH_7_bM09aglyiZeqS7yB3wxagOi0bAaGXFCQp0HHNI3bbNybgWc_g\\u003dw1376-h768\",1376,768],\"Title slide contrasting the past of chatbots (speech bubble) with the present of agents (robot executing code on a computer).\",\"HOW AI AGENTS WORK (AND HOW TO BUILD ONE)\\nDeconstructing Claude Code: From Chatbot to Digital Intern.\\n\\n[The slide is split into two main sections with dashed lines, labeled 'The Past' and 'The Present'.]\\n\\nThe Past: Chatbots\\n[Icon of a speech bubble with three dots, surrounded by dashed lines representing measurement/bounding box.]\\n\\nThe Present: Agents\\n[Illustration of a robot sitting at a desk interacting with a computer setup.]\\n[The monitor shows Python code executing a client call to Anthropic and an array of messages.]\\n[Arrows point from the scene to action labels:]\\n- EXECUTE CODE\\n- ACCESS FILE SYSTEM\\n- PERFORM TASKS\\n- EXECUTE CODE (pointed at the keyboard)\\n\\nMost people use AI to ask questions. But what if the AI could use a computer the way you do? We are going to deconstruct \\\"Claude Code\\\"—a powerful coding agent—and learn how to build a digital coworker from scratch using Python.\\n\\n\\u003e python build_agent.py\"],[[\"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/notebooklm/AG60hOqCc-lNDD-fcvKyVfTaaBcEdu18Z9RH4loH1UvEvrz2-_zrKelUJdfB70wAc4M6T-irv6uiO9xUpq0C1e7D461AlUKSTDHxU8K1B72oIke7CLwaFf35tKWjRSEJgTOIjAlth1QbREJ_BwKSuMg3eld94zoeIkHvPFg_XC8\\u003dw1376-h768\",1376,768],\"Diagram comparing Thinking (Passive Chatbot, a brain in a jar) to Doing (Active Agent, a robot with an Observe-Act-Think loop).\",\"THE DIFFERENCE BETWEEN THINKING AND DOING\\n\\nTHINKING (Passive)\\n\\n[Illustration shows a user asking a QUESTION to a Passive (Chatbot), depicted as a geometric brain sealed in a glass jar, which then provides an ANSWER.]\\n\\nPassive (Chatbot)\\n\\nSpace Mono\\nCHATBOT\\n- Like a wise librarian who can only talk.\\n- Takes a User Question -\\u003e Gives an Answer.\\n- Cannot see files or run programs.\\n\\nDOING (Active)\\n\\n[Illustration shows an Active (Agent) robot holding a wrench and a file folder.]\\n[An orange loop around the robot shows the flow: THINK, ACT, OBSERVE.]\\n\\nActive (Agent)\\n\\nSpace Mono\\nAGENT\\n- Like a research intern who can fetch books and file reports.\\n- Takes a User Goal -\\u003e Thinks -\\u003e Acts -\\u003e Observes -\\u003e Achieves Result.\"],[[\"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/notebooklm/AG60hOpjlsS93P-quCKDLgSZGXcaxml9Ea7ahlx2Jg_FsrntWyqHztUbMfDLFvND-19zdajmrC64tMXS9WYaffEUfyl2OSl6o87vjj98_W2kmSCT30cNv65E-boIFxNzu2cDnJIBPFq2nySNeEPt_mrQQSQ9jtuBSmrXTIBUgg\\u003dw1376-h768\",1376,768],\"Diagram illustrating the Agent Loop: AI progresses through continuous cycles of THINK, ACT (call a tool), and OBSERVE (get results).\",\"THE SECRET SAUCE: THE AGENT LOOP\\n\\n[Diagram shows a continuous circular flow labeled THE LOOP.]\\n\\nTHINK\\n[Icon of a brain]\\nAI decides next step based on history.\\n\\n[Arrow points from THINK to ACT.]\\n\\nACT\\n[Icon of a command prompt]\\nAI calls a Tool (e.g., run command).\\n\\n[Arrow points from ACT to OBSERVE.]\\n\\nOBSERVE\\n[Icon of a document with charts]\\nComputer returns result (Success/Error).\\n\\n[Arrow points from OBSERVE back to THINK.]\\n\\n[Code snippet in a window:]\\nwhile True:\\ndecision \\u003d model(history)\\nif decision \\u003d\\u003d \\\"stop\\\": break\\nresult \\u003d execute_tool(decision)\\nhistory.append(result)\\n# The Agent Loop in Python\\n\\nThe Model is 80%. Code is 20%. The \\\"magic\\\" is just a while loop that runs until the job is done.\"],[[\"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/notebooklm/AG60hOrLVyoPau2O6z8q1eVDpGbfWe7PZSwqYAp4mYjAx7RXWa3Wrg3viSlxSAXp26XC1wIeYsK5iAXxBC9kwm7ZMN5YIsWlRuNH9PVdkguB4xq6YRrJ32fsjtDhLl4NarxAwwTbDYFkrVQsapFyKeP4zo-py8emjZyo4J4Jj-Q\\u003dw1376-h768\",1376,768],\"Illustration of a high-tech Swiss Army knife detailing the four core tools an AI Agent uses: bash, read_file, write_file, and edit_file.\",\"THE AGENT'S UTILITY BELT\\n\\n[Illustration of a high-tech circuit-themed Swiss Army knife with four tools extended.]\\n\\nbash\\n- Runs terminal commands. Installs software, moves files, runs scripts.\\n\\nread_file\\n- Allows the agent to see code or documents.\\n\\nwrite_file\\n- Creates new files or overwrites existing ones.\\n\\nedit_file\\n- Makes surgical changes to specific parts of a file.\\n\\nTakeaway: With just these four tools, an agent can build software, write essays, or analyze data.\"],[[\"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/notebooklm/AG60hOpVmdhR0eCFytgjTJPUeRWxk7DygJujgLLl59FEMXwaANi5EHlJKvaS607HqpOJChbKmtUP1tIX3LSnOF8pnnktftV1_y2lxC-lbUcsuoo4FBZIryr8XILg0c9tctcGloKTFHIgpWlH2iCY0ODvCrNvLFeyLMG_4QPk6gs\\u003dw1376-h768\",1376,768],\"Terminal demonstration of Claude Code refactoring a login system, showing the agent using read_file, bash, and edit_file tools sequentially.\",\"SEE IT IN ACTION: CLAUDE CODE\\n\\n[Terminal output log showing the agent executing a complex task.]\\n\\nUser: Refactor the login system to use secure tokens.\\n\\u003e Agent is thinking...\\n\\u003e read_file src/login.py Reading 50 lines...\\n\\u003e bash pip install jwt Requirement already satisfied...\\n\\u003e edit_file src/login.py Replacing lines 12-15...\\n\\u003e bash python test_login.py Tests passed.\\n\\u003e Done. The login system now uses JWT tokens.\\n\\n[Annotation text]: Real-world automation. Tasks that take 20 minutes are done in seconds.\"],[[\"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/notebooklm/AG60hOroUBHDPldLNRywcTeye7bzfGvLpFxA912uw_6rY9IyMbSh-LPRpXWVBmqvqI9q7c-0JidxNw_sY7S_BCV1lQiRFoPTYa43OoookLPRGyP9PvE13ez-YS3N2APkz6Ss8CIeHzAcq5zHzV2NeJnDr-U3KWbnUa39b0L5Jw\\u003dw1376-h768\",1376,768],\"A roadmap showing the five levels of agent development, from Level 0 (Skeleton/50 lines) to Level 4 (Expert/550 lines).\",\"THE BUILDER'S JOURNEY: LEVEL 0 TO LEVEL 4\\n\\n[Flow diagram showing an upward path through five development stages.]\\n\\nThe Skeleton\\nLevel 0\\n- Bash Only\\n- (~50 lines)\\n\\nThe Body\\nLevel 1\\n- Core Tools\\n- (~200 lines)\\n\\nThe Planner\\nLevel 2\\n- Memory/Todo\\n- (~300 lines)\\n\\nThe Manager\\nLevel 3\\n- Subagents\\n- (~450 lines)\\n\\nThe Expert\\nLevel 4\\n- Skills/Knowledge\\n- (~550 lines)\\n\\nWe are going to virtually build this agent line-by-line. Starting with a 50-line skeleton, we will upgrade it to solve specific problems like \\\"forgetfulness\\\" and \\\"messiness\\\".\"],[[\"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/notebooklm/AG60hOqipK8BaQznIoATnejSEGcijXvm4PvAheOPLBckLPtaVQUd27hU6nI2T6qkOw4SzH5MfQQzolaRg6hAtulttEoIOExX4LWtk5KGrWBHaHxPI3XP8ZtjkfZNDo_lu-DQm1QPoUAa05H1Niq6-qryzkc0lz88CFrEGTePVDo\\u003dw1376-h768\",1376,768],\"Level 0: The Skeleton, defined by 50 lines of code and a single Bash tool, using the \\\"Inception Trick\\\" to run itself as a sub-agent.\",\"Level 0: The Skeleton (Bash is All You Need)\\n\\nTHE SPECS\\n- Lines of Code: ~50\\n- Tools: 1 (Bash)\\n- Philosophy: If you can run a command, you can do anything.\\n\\nTHE VISUALIZATION\\n[Illustration of a robotic arm poised over an ENTER key.]\\n\\n- THE INCEPTION TRICK\\n- [Flow diagram showing recursion]: Agent -\\u003e bash [Arrow] python agent.py (Sub-agent) -\\u003e bash [Arrow] Result\\n\\n\\\"The essence of an agent is ONE tool + ONE loop.\\\"\"],[[\"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/notebooklm/AG60hOrbW9tCA5UKBc6AcrrMvVqIeWyC6J8wZMHKR0EF-qzKVh5MwdfdqHRAruovUBkjo-nHpK2EcKhRp1hkUUMcD-JpBYc0YYzWIoPlsd2rtar6AMDdZEspDMWsGZfuONWvG6gelZywpHBwdnGOJ3AsnNS-IFyISQsuMQxuGM8\\u003dw1376-h768\",1376,768],\"Level 1: The Body, where the agent gains file reading and writing tools and the core Model-Tool-Result loop logic is established.\",\"Level 1: The Body (Model as Agent)\\n\\nTHE SPECS\\n- Lines of Code: ~200\\n- New Tools: read_file, write_file, edit_file\\n- Why Upgrade? Safer, surgical edits.\\n\\nTHE VISUALIZATION\\n[Illustration of a futuristic humanoid robot holding a document.]\\n\\nTHE LOOP LOGIC\\n[Flow diagram of the core agent loop.]\\nUser -\\u003e Model -\\u003e Tool -\\u003e Result -\\u003e [Asterisk showing loop back to Model] -\\u003e Final Response\\nCritical: The model loops repeatedly until the task is complete.\\n\\n\\\"The body is built. Now it can see and touch the world (read \\u0026 write).\\\"\"],[[\"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/notebooklm/AG60hOo7Dk4L3dVIsm55OYeXQSlwgsHhtE6p-_Kx8XuWJ_9w7tutAs1h-7DIoBvBdy92yz72TrxuRoZBnYo9SzGvHPnL6Vmy9tO1RWmNE71g27i7VEBlNfCbfKKzRIkI9Ep_76UKSwOSeCfxxvpDDfAFXgPXMiYhNhNGza9ay5w\\u003dw1376-h768\",1376,768],\"Level 2: The Planner, which cures forgetfulness by introducing the Todo Tool, forcing the AI to maintain a checklist for focus.\",\"Level 2: The Planner (Curing Amnesia)\\n\\nTHE SPECS\\n- Problem: On complex tasks, the AI forgets the plan or gets distracted.\\n\\nTHE VISUALIZATION\\n[Illustration of a humanoid robot holding a clipboard.]\\n\\nTHE UPGRADE: The Todo Tool\\n[Image showing a digital checklist on a clipboard.]\\n- Step 1: Analyze database [Checked]\\n- Step 2: Write migration script in_progress [Highlighted]\\n- Step 3: Run tests [Unchecked]\\n\\n\\\"Structure constrains AND enables. By forcing the AI to write a checklist, we prevent hallucination and keep it focused.\\\"\"],[[\"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/notebooklm/AG60hOr-HKXrr-cg4XdyxFfk0L227zh9x8t9VOF_6Dns2dZif6tHr8do_xwxG7btRBboYjmKfYrtl3iaShGEo2GMGrTZUtciuWzqmdENFGpWqZW57g3Y8TNB62-dXnV1Q1epYRKyATC4IhLoswsJd6eFLPycsWrJhB8-NbSrXrk\\u003dw1376-h768\",1376,768],\"Level 3: The Manager, which solves Context Pollution by using The Task Tool to spawn specialized subagents like Researcher Bot and Coder Bot.\",\"Level 3: The Manager (Divide and Conquer)\\n\\nProblem: Context Pollution. Too much text in history confuses the AI.\\n\\n[Diagram shows the Main Agent connecting to two specialized Subagents.]\\n\\nMain Agent [Icon of a central robot]\\n\\nResearcher Bot [Icon of a robot researching at a desktop]\\n- Runs in fresh window.\\n- Returns 1 sentence summary.\\n\\nCoder Bot [Icon of a robot coding]\\n- Executes task.\\n- Returns result.\\n\\nThe Task Tool:\\nSpawns a subagent to do a specific job, keeping the Main Agent's memory clean and focused.\"],[[\"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/notebooklm/AG60hOqJngsyoD9KqIwxd0gU_ChhlKg3qVy_uATs5ZIZ1OrdAkutlk8WUdj9yezXEAOUdFprfmP2oVA24gXBnApm_B8DWSzKJlXshPIenYtlXT_Xg6_ekQV_AXcWKSFgJ4QdU38_vn2E3F4TSycclO3KCyUaMVmaflACzXo1a_k\\u003dw1376-h768\",1376,768],\"Level 4: The Expert introduces plug-and-play Skills, written in text files like SKILL.md, allowing the agent to gain instant expertise without retraining.\",\"Level 4: The Expert (The Matrix Moment)\\n\\n[Illustration of a humanoid robot inserting a floppy disk labeled \\\"PDF Skill\\\" into a slot on its head.]\\n\\nTHE SPECS\\n- Tools \\u003d Hands (What the model CAN do.)\\n- Skills \\u003d Knowledge (How the model KNOWS to do it.)\\n\\nTHE UPGRADE: Instant Expertise\\n- Problem: You can't train an AI on every niche topic.\\n- Solution: \\\"Skills\\\". You write a text file (SKILL.md) with instructions. The agent reads it and instantly becomes an expert in that topic. No training required.\\n\\nSkills are plug-and-play knowledge modules. They allow the AI to adapt to any domain *without* being retraining.\"],[[\"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/notebooklm/AG60hOr21kTLS-QAjFJTgdPi7Mglr8XVNsr5aEarNQpac29g9LZqnxegqDVuwwBFn9m8zSuyMvOtYdBC09sZsXTjTR_uve8-G0Z2je51xB4WrVLMF1XCfGb6RmGPyWjO1JNhH8XF78yQu3xnT3I5XrbDSBvXDXmzwJM4TqOF-mI\\u003dw1376-h768\",1376,768],\"Comparison showing the shift from expensive Traditional AI Training (server racks) to cost-free, fast AI Skills (writing instructions in SKILL.md).\",\"From Training to Teaching\\n\\nTraditional AI Training\\n[Illustration of large server racks.]\\n- Cost: $10,000+. Time: Weeks. Requirement: PhD + GPU Cluster.\\n\\nAI Skills\\n[Illustration of a monitor displaying instructions within a file named SKILL.md.]\\n- Cost: $0. Time: 5 Minutes. Requirement: English Language.\\n- SKILL.md content:\\n- # AI Skill: Data Analysis\\n- ## Instructions\\n- 1. Read the CSV file.\\n- 2. Calculate the average.\\n- 3. Summarize the findings in a report.\\n\\nDemocratization: You don't need to be an engineer to customize an agent. You just need to be able to write clear instructions.\"],[[\"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/notebooklm/AG60hOqpSaIwFx-JiuwSh5Ak6zGGA9xogHeBIytNsoVo5-kEr7Lgnj4RJnIEoyGJ3_5ZeniywJwaoCMp7lsjgjTqvnov3W-uQLq8Kq86vizTiZVSYiMjeYT593qDgp0nhhb1RW3u7kKX8NDYEoPADbQcBM1aOfEBSxKHnz3iYoY\\u003dw1376-h768\",1376,768],\"Schematic showing a human conductor orchestrating an agent orchestra, highlighting trends like Managing, Agent Teams, and Hyper-Personalization.\",\"The Future of Agents\\n\\nHUMAN CONDUCTOR \\u0026 AGENT ORCHESTRA (SCHEMATIC VIEW)\\n[Illustration showing a human figure standing on a podium directing many specialized robots (Build Agent, Research Agent, Analyst Agent) that are working collaboratively at desks.]\\n\\nTRENDS.md\\n// TRENDS.md - The Future of Agents\\n\\u003e From Chatting to Managing:\\n- Humans will define goals, agents will execute the work.\\n- _Moving beyond simple Q\\u0026A to autonomous task completion_\\n\\n\\u003e Agent Teams:\\n- Agents will hire Subagents to solve problems we didn't anticipate.\\n- _Collaborative swarms for complex, multi-step challenges_\\n\\n\\u003e Hyper-Personalization:\\n- Your agent will be unique, loaded with your custom Skills.\\n- _A personal digital extension, perfectly aligned with your needs_\"],[[\"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/notebooklm/AG60hOpKqM1aaIm9F17DAa0lIbDRGOgoXhWwLOkWtCQ-fVGFYNPNwTLl6DxVkZJzHRMExSaQgzzvAw_IVQ3LrzuYkXdH6Gh4Xydvfdl5E6ZraVgfYPWi7zYftneOvRJ-Ax6VtN4znLuPrcQu6NpYmFTPGni1g_kOJe1BQBttmzI\\u003dw1376-h768\",1376,768],\"Final slide encouraging building, providing the GitHub repository link and noting that the Level 0 agent is only 50 lines of Python code.\",\"Start Building\\n\\nSTARTER KIT (ISOMETRIC VIEW)\\n[Illustration of a box labeled Starter Kit containing symbols for Python, an Anthropic API Key, and GitHub.]\\n\\nCODE.md\\n- Repository: github.com/shareAI-lab/learn-claude-code\\n- Prerequisites: Python basics + API Key\\n\\nThe Level 0 agent is only 50 lines of code. Download it. Break it. Rebuild it.\\nThe model is the brain. The code is just the way we let it out. What will you build?\"]],\"https://contribution.usercontent.google.com/download?c\\u003dCgpub3RlYm9va2xtEkYSD2FydGlmYWN0c19tZWRpYRozCiQ5NmZhMjQwZS1hZDM2LTRjZjgtODhhMi01MmUxOGIzYjNmMjUSCxIHEOXwnYDTCxgB\\u0026filename\\u003dBuilding_Digital_Workers.pdf\\u0026opi\\u003d96797242\"],null,null,1],[\"9ca94236-8568-4b24-bde1-fa7bc48db6de\",\"Think Act Observe The Agent Loop\",1,[[[\"a474cd35-6c21-4e72-94a0-c38b5491b449\"]],[[\"d4d1f671-0cd1-493a-bbf4-f64e9d6d42e6\"]],[[\"735fcfef-9fbd-4c89-9789-6a9760587bec\"]],[[\"4d3f7b07-e9e6-43d3-ab8b-184fa27a9f1e\"]],[[\"a5ec927b-12eb-45d1-989f-12eb3db4ce53\"]],[[\"c361a555-5c2d-42e2-94d0-a65da95be660\"]],[[\"48f71a82-08d3-46fa-a37f-d657fb2f0723\"]],[[\"d6ce2ec3-f98a-4529-acd5-08bff271cb3b\"]],[[\"ef358221-3904-4dbc-be6f-e1e8dea63954\"]]],3,null,[null,[\"Create an engaging podcast for high school students (ages 14-18) explaining how Claude Code and AI agents work. Use simple language, relatable analogies (like comparing agents to video game NPCs or virtual assistants), and make it fun and educational. Cover: 1) What is an AI agent vs regular AI, 2) The agent loop (think-act-observe), 3) Tools and how agents use them, 4) How Claude Code specifically helps programmers, 5) Future possibilities.
